raw_consumer_statement:
I terminated a domestic employee ( nanny ) who had been issued a companion card on my American Express account for employment-related purposes only. Upon termination, her authorization to use the card ended. I was not aware she had retained the physical card. She subsequently made unauthorized charges totaling approximately {$6000.00} after her termination date. I filed a police report when I discovered the charges. 

I filed a fraud dispute with American Express. The claim was initially approved, but then ultimately denied. The denial letter stated that because I " provided my account number to a merchant, '' I authorized the charges. This reasoning is factually incorrect. I did not provide my account number to any merchant. I issued a companion card to an employee for employment purposes. That authorization was conditional on her continued employment and terminated the moment her employment ended. Any charges made after her termination date are unauthorized by definition. 

American Express 's own XXXX liability policy covers unauthorized use. These charges are unauthorized use. The denial misapplies their policy by conflating issuing a companion card to an employee with voluntarily providing account information to a merchant. These are legally and factually distinct situations. 

I called to appeal and was told the case has been reopened. I am filing this complaint to create a formal record and to request that the CFPB ensure American Express properly reviews this claim under the Fair Credit Billing Act and their own XXXX liability policy. I am seeking reversal of the {$4500.00} debit that was applied to my account after the initial credit was reversed. 

A police report has been filed regarding the unauthorized use of my card by this former employee.
